Automakers push EV sales as $7,500 US tax credit is set to end DETROIT (Reuters) -Automakers are urging customers to snap up electric vehicles before a $7,500 U.S. tax credit goes away this fall.  The same day, Ford Motor extended its deal for free home chargers and installation through the end of September to entice EV shoppers.  Sweeping tax and budget legislation approved by Congress will eliminate $7,500 tax credits for buying or leasing new electric vehicles and a $4,000 used-EV credit at the end of September.
